_description: 5-year-old child with VSD planned for surgical repair. Tests PAEDIATRIC_DEVICE flag (Phase 8), paediatric implant age check (Phase 3), and cardiac surgery package matching (SV/MC specialty).

clinical.chief_complaints: Recurrent chest infections since birth, failure to thrive, heart murmur detected at birth, breathlessness on exertion
clinical.history_of_present_illness: 5-year-old male child with murmur detected at birth. Parents report recurrent respiratory infections (4-5 episodes per year). Child has poor weight gain (failure to thrive). Breathlessness on moderate exertion. Echocardiogram 6 months ago showed moderate VSD with left to right shunt. Cardiologist advised surgical repair before school age. Now admitted electively for VSD closure.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Ventricular septal defect (VSD) — perimembranous, moderate size, with left to right shunt, haemodynamically significant
clinical.planned_procedure: Surgical VSD closure under cardiopulmonary bypass

examination_findings.general: Thin, malnourished-appearing child. Weight 14 kg (below 3rd centile for age). Height 102 cm.
examination_findings.cvs: Pansystolic murmur grade 4/6 at left sternal border. Thrill palpable. Active precordium.
examination_findings.rs: Bilateral clear air entry. No added sounds.
examination_findings.abdomen: Soft, liver 2cm BCM (RVF signs), no splenomegaly
examination_findings.cns: Alert, playful, developmentally appropriate for age

investigations.result_summary: 2D Echo: Perimembranous VSD 8mm, left to right shunt. LA/LV enlarged. Pulmonary pressure estimated 45 mmHg. No other structural defect. LVEF 62%.
ECG: Sinus rhythm, LVH pattern, P-mitrale, QRS 0.08s
Chest X-ray: Cardiomegaly (CTR 0.58), pulmonary plethora, prominent pulmonary vasculature

clinical.past_medical_history: Recurrent respiratory infections since birth. Murmur since birth. Failure to thrive.
clinical.family_history: No family history of congenital heart disease

non_clinical_documents_in_hand.label: Photo of patient on hospital bed

clinical.notes: Paediatric cardiac surgery case. Age 5 years = paediatric implant/device rules apply. Cardiac bypass circuit and consumables are paediatric-sized. Anaesthesia has been counselled on paediatric considerations.
